## [00:00 - 03:00]

So what I want to show you here is how I take my process from kind of test to prospecting, and then what I do to stabilize and what I'm looking for in my numbers. So in this particular ad account, we've been through graduation testing, so we've been into phase one. We've done our audience testing to find opportunities, as shown in the grad testing part of the training. We've then gone through phase two to find ads that work and that resonate, and you can see the ROAS numbers generally as we're going through, and then we're into phase three. Now by phase three, we're looking to build stability and consistency with our numbers. So these are just looking at the stats, I think for the last three weeks, give or take, and it just shows you the kind of progression through grad testing. So what we've done here is we've graduated four or three audiences, so a lookalike 1%, interests and broad, and actually there's a few different, two different campaigns created, one for a new landing page after which we launched broad, and you can see the results of what we're doing. But what we've done is although we're seeing good ROAS, we're working on optimizing and stabilizing the numbers before we go ahead with increasing budgets. And without foul, when we take these processes in place and we build stability and optimization right in early on, scaling becomes so much easier. We don't need to use manual bid, we don't need to use hacks and launch 30 different ad sets and start and stop and all these crazy roles because we're following a defined process. So at this stage, we've now been running the ads for two to three weeks, we've done lots of optimization, including we've tested landing pages and we've found a winning variant for this particular website. And it's converting better. And as you can see with this interest campaign, they're exactly the same, except one has a new landing page and ROAS is so much better. So they're the kind of things that we're doing to optimize and stabilize. And in particular, this one, we've launched it and we wanna build that stability again, optimize it, tweak it, and then be at a stage where we can push more budget and be a lot more aggressive. So when I'm looking at this, one thing I'm looking at is what that kind of progression looks like, just to demonstrate for you how we've kind of taken this. So if I break it out by week, you can see how the performance has progressed through the work that we've put in. So this is in ascending date order. So this campaign here, for example, it started off in week one as a ROAS of 2.06, in week two, it's 2.2. That for me is a real positive signal that I've gone into my second week and I'm able to maintain a plus, two ROAS plus performance.

## [03:00 - 05:07]

On this ad account, our baseline, our minimum is to aim at 1, 1.5 ROAS. Anything above two is really good. Really, we wanna push it to 2.53 and there's many days and weeks that we've managed to do that, many days at least. But that's the kind of step process. Like once you know what your target is, get there, maintain it, improve it. You can maintain it with your optimizations, with your ad spend, with your pixel, all the kind of details that go in before you start getting aggressive with your spend. This campaign here are prospecting phase three with a new landing page. And we can see here that it's not progressed as much as our broad. So there's more that we want to do to get this CPA down. For this particular campaign, the AOV is really kind of helping us that we've helped to push it up, but we need to get our costs down. So that's the next step for these particular campaigns. And you can see down here, we're actually seeing a much better progression for our lookalike campaign. So we've done some optimizations and tweaks, which we'll cover off in the other videos, to look at how we can get our costs down. And the cost for this lookalike campaign is crushing interest and broad, but it fluctuates. And we wanna get stability. We wanna make sure that we can maintain this three ROAS, which so far with a $1,300 a week spend, we're able to, but we wanna do even more. We wanna continue that, maintain it, and then we start increasing budgets. And one of the things about this is identifying where the positive opportunities are, where you can actually get better performance. So all this kind of data is useful for me to know where I am and where I'm heading before I can make the next correct decisions. And in the next video, I'll go through how I go deeper into finding pockets of opportunity to expand and improve my performance so that I can actually identify where I'm wasting spend and maybe where I'm underspending where the opportunities actually exist.
